subtasks.description: Query TPC-H to identify top 10 customers by lifetime value in BUILDING market segment. Return customer_id, customer_name, total_lifetime_value, and order_count for ranking and context.

Query TPC-H to retrieve detailed order pattern metrics for the top 10 customers: order frequency, average order value, order date ranges, product categories ordered, and seasonal trends. Include customer_id to match with ranking results.

For each of the top 10 customers, synthesize a personalized retention narrative that references their specific order patterns (frequency, value trends, product preferences, seasonality). Combine customer LTV context with order behavior insights to create compelling, data-driven retention messaging.

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks required: (1) Top 10 customer ranking by LTV in BUILDING segment requires a multi-table join and aggregation (mid-tier sql_gen). (2) Order pattern analysis requires a separate query with window functions and aggregations across orders table (mid-tier sql_gen). These are split because they query different granularities (customer summary vs. order detail). (3) Narrative generation synthesizes ranked customer data with behavioral patterns to produce personalized retention messaging, requiring cross_recon (mid-tier) to combine insights and generate contextual narratives. No policy_action needed—this is contextual synthesis, not governance evaluation. No multitool_plan needed—this uses only SQL and synthesis. Mid-tier assigned to cross_recon because it must intelligently weave customer LTV and order patterns into coherent, personalized narratives."

output.sql: SELECT c.c_custkey AS customer_id, c.c_name AS customer_name, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS total_lifetime_value, COUNT(o.o_orderkey) AS order_count FROM customer c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY total_lifetime_value DESC LIMIT 10

SELECT c.c_custkey AS customer_id, c.c_name, COUNT(DISTINCT o.o_orderkey) AS order_frequency, AVG(o.o_totalprice) AS avg_order_value, MIN(o.o_orderdate) AS first_order_date, MAX(o.o_orderdate) AS last_order_date, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM o.o_orderdate)::INT AS order_year, COUNT(DISTINCT EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM o.o_orderdate)) AS distinct_quarters_ordered, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS lifetime_value FROM customer c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC LIMIT 10

reconciled.sql_value: INNER JOIN on c_custkey aggregating total_lifetime_value DESC
reconciled.note: No MongoDB operational state provided; SQL is authoritative source

retention_narratives.personalized_narrative: UNABLE_TO_GENERATE - No actual query results provided in upstream_outputs. The SQL queries are defined but not executed. To generate retention narratives, please execute: (1) sql_top_customers query to identify top 10 BUILDING segment customers by LTV, (2) sql_order_patterns query to extract frequency, value trends, and temporal patterns. Then cross-reference with any MongoDB operational metrics if available.
recommended_actions: Execute SQL queries against TPC-H database
Extract order frequency distribution (orders per quarter/year)
Calculate order value trend (growth/decline over time)
Identify product category preferences from line items
Match with MongoDB customer engagement metrics if available
Generate individual narratives with specific order references

required_sql_enrichment: l_productkey, l_quantity, l_extendedprice JOIN lineitem to identify preferred product categories
p_name, p_category JOIN part to categorize product preferences
DATE_PART analysis on o_orderdate for seasonality detection
Year-over-year order value comparison for trend analysis
data_requirements_for_narratives.narrative_generation_logic: For each top-10 customer: (1) LTV Position - state their rank and total value, (2) Order Rhythm - cite frequency pattern and avg order size, (3) Value Trajectory - describe trend (growing/stable/declining), (4) Product Loyalty - reference top 2-3 preferred categories, (5) Seasonality Insight - note peak ordering quarters, (6) Recency Signal - assess engagement level, (7) Risk/Opportunity Assessment - classify as at_risk/stable/growing, (8) Personalized CTA - recommend specific retention action
